title: Proclamation on Countering Cartel Criminal Activity
summary: A presidential proclamation describing the United States' ongoing effort to dismantle cartels and foreign terrorist organizations operating in the Western Hemisphere. The proclamation notes designation of certain cartels and transnational gangs as foreign terrorist organizations and outlines related measures and authorities to counter cartel-associated criminal activity.

title: Proclamation on Domestic Data Center Infrastructure and Ratepayer Protections
summary: This proclamation states that the United States’ economic and technological leadership depends on reliable, large-scale data center infrastructure built domestically. It notes data centers are foundational to the internet, cloud computing, and artificial intelligence, and it outlines objectives and measures to promote domestic data center development while protecting ratepayers from potential costs. The proclamation connects these goals to national economic and security interests.

title: Nomination of Kevin Warsh to be Chairman of the Federal Reserve Board for a four-year term and to serve as a Governor for a fourteen-year term beginning February 1, 2026
summary: The White House announces the nomination of Kevin Warsh of Florida to serve as Chairman of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System for a four-year term and to serve as a member of the Board for a fourteen-year term beginning February 1, 2026. The announcement appears in the White House 'Nominations Sent to the Senate' post published in March 2026.

title: National Angel Family Day, 2026 Proclamation
summary: A presidential proclamation designating National Angel Family Day for 2026. The proclamation describes the purpose as remembering and honoring victims associated with crimes attributed to individuals unauthorized to reside in the United States and with drugs trafficked across the U.S. border, and expresses support for Angel Families. It calls for observance by the federal government and acknowledgement of affected families.
